___ the throat with the thumb and fingers, making the universal choking sign indicates the need for help when a victim is choking.

The ___ are found in an area behind the abdominal wall.

Opioids are medications used primarily for pain relief, common examples are ___, morphine, and fentanyl.
Shock is a life-threatening condition that occurs when the circulatory system can't ___ adequate blood flow.

The abdominal cavity contains the liver and part of the ___ intestine.
When assessing circulation for a responsive adult patient you should assess the ___ pulse.
Skin that is ___ in color is called cyanotic.
A patient who presents with normal vital signs and shows no indications of life-threatening problems may be described as ___.

When the ___ is absent is a situation where it would be appropriate to place an angulated extremity back into the anatomical position.

The most appropriate care for an open abdominal injury is to cover the wound with a ___, sterile dressing.
A ratio of ___ compressions per minute and a depth of at least 2 inches are the rate and depth for chest compressions on an adult.
When more rescuers arrive on scene you should assign tasks to other rescuers and rotate compressors every ___ or more frequently if needed to avoid fatigue.
___ is an important tool for identifying whether opioids may be involved in a life-threatening emergency.
___ is a life-threatening condition that occurs when the circulatory system can't maintain adequate blood flow.

choices.text: Affect the rate of diffusion of a gas by ventilation.

choices.text: At any dead end or face area.

choices.text: Review the rescue team's findings after they have returned from underground.

questions.text: Class A fires are those that involve:

choices.text: Ordinary combustible materials such as wood, plastics, paper, and cloth.

questions.text: A monoammonium phosphate extinguisher is effective in fighting:

choices.text: Outline the body with chalk or paint on the floor.

Report the location to the command center.

questions.text: A mine rescue team could remove standing water from an unventilated area.

choices.text: If gas conditions permit, using non-conductive suction line and a pump set up in fresh air.
Standing water can not be pumped from an unventilated area.
